preamble: Raising of the open-mid front vowel [ɛ] to the close-mid front vowel [e] is a frequent process in historical phonology. This Raising can occur as a systematic shift in stressed syllables or as a step in a larger chain shift within the front vowel series.

examples.note: Latin short /e/ (open /ɛ/) often raised to closed /e/ in certain Romance environments or dialects.
While Spanish often diphthongized [ɛ], in closed syllables or under other conditions, Raising to [e] occurred in many Romance varieties.
Historical Raising of the long open-mid vowel towards the close-mid and eventually high-front position (iotacism).
Stage of the Great Vowel Shift where Middle English long open-mid /ɛ:/ raised to /e:/ before further raising to /i:/.

sources: Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
Campbell, L. (2013). Historical Linguistics: An Introduction.
Hock, H. H. (1991). Principles of Historical Linguistics.
